This is a list of every One Piece character that has ever appeared in or been mentioned in the manga and has been named (sometimes the names are only given in one of the databooks or an SBS). This list includes animals, zombies and numbered groups of unnamed people. Note that some names have alternate spellings, which are not included on this list.

Contents

  • 1 Individuals
  • 2 Groups of known numbers
  • 3 See also
  • 4 Site Navigation

Quick Answers

Who are some of the most popular One Piece characters?
Luffy, Zoro, and Sanji consistently rank as the most popular characters in One Piece. Other characters such as Chopper, Sabo, Ace, Nami, Usopp, Robin, Franky, and Brook also enjoy significant popularity among fans.
Provided by: Fandom
What are some unique traits of One Piece anime characters?
One Piece anime characters, such as Monkey D. Luffy, exhibit distinctive traits. Luffy is characterized by his fearlessness, unwavering self-belief, and lack of common sense. He is optimistic, often viewing situations positively. His traits include adventurous spirit, carefree nature, simple-mindedness, leadership, charisma, loyalty, selflessness, respect, compassion, emotional nature, righteousness, respect for food, confidence, stubbornness, and humility.
Provided by: Fandom
Can you name some of the One Piece characters who are animals?
The most significant animal among the One Piece cast is Tony Tony Chopper, the doctor of the Straw Hat Pirates, who is a reindeer that gained a human transformation from a Devil Fruit. Other animals include Karoo (a super spot-billed duck), Chouchou (a dog), Pappag (a starfish) and Farul (a horse).
Provided by: Community
Who are some of the One Piece characters that have alternate spellings for their names?
Several One Piece characters have alternate name spellings due to translation and transliteration variations. Examples include 'Fran' as an English equivalent for 'Flam', 'Brook' and 'Brooke' with the former being the confirmed version, and 'Jinbe' and 'Jimbei' as seen in the Viz English manga and Funimation dub.
Provided by: Fandom
